outcome_text: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are used to inform the risk management process.
scoring_text: Ability to integrate AI measurement results into risk management decision-making, including translating trustworthiness measurements into risk management actions, updating risk assessments based on measurement findings, and ensuring measurement data flows into GOVERN, MAP, and MANAGE function activities.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to integrate AI measurement results into risk management decision-making, including translating trustworthiness measurements into risk management actions, updating risk assessments based on measurement findings, and ensuring measurement data flows into GOVERN, MAP, and MANAGE function activities.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-4.2 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.686, cosine: 0.696). Secondary: RM-S-001 (RM, cosine: 0.674), RC-T-012 (RC, cosine: 0.662).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.686). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessments, risk]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, actions, activities, based, data, decision] vs WIDAI terms [bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation, harm] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.854, R=0.884). R exceeds P by 0.031 — the WIDAI KSA's 20 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 37 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:2.0%, N:67.4%, C:30.6%). No dominant NLI classification — the logical relationship between the risk management outcome and WIDAI KSA is ambiguous.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.696 ≈ STS 0.686 (gap 0.010). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, assessments, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessments, risk]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, actions, activities, based, data, decision]. WIDAI-only: [bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation, harm].
